content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
· 1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Leticia Ramirez 
Interim Tracy City Attorney 
333 Civic Center Plaza 
Tracy, CA 95376 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-19-102 
Dear Ms. Ramirez: 
June 18, 2019 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Tracy City Councilmember 
Rhodesia Ransom regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act"). 1 Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Act and not under other conflict of interest laws. Also note that we are not a finder of fact when 
rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your 
facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions 
sh<?uld change, you should contact us for additional.advice. 
QUESTIONS 
(1) Does the Act prohibit Councilmember Ransom, in her private capacity as Executive 
Director of the local nonprofit organization "Sow A Seed Community Foundation," from executing 
a lease agreement for office and programming space for the Foundation with the Housing Authority 
of the County of San Joaquin? 
(2) If the Foundation enters into the lease agreement with the Housing Authority, would the 
Councilmember be disqualified from future City Council decisions involving the Housing 
Authority? 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 


CONCLUSIONS 
File No. A-19-102 
Page No. 2 
(1) No. The Act would not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the Housing 
Authority's governmental decisions relating to the lease agreement as the Executive Director of the 
Foundation because the Councilmember would not be "making," "participating in," or "using 
official position to attempt to influence" those decisions so long as she does not purport to act 
within her authority as a Tracy City Councilmember or on behalf of the Tracy City Council in 
regard to those decisions. 
(2) The Act's conflict of interest provisions apply on a case by case basis, and would 
prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in a governmental decision if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision would have a material financial effect on one or more of her financial 
interests distinguishable from the decision's effect on the public generally. Accordingly, the 
Councilmember should seek additional advice if there is any indication that a specifically identified 
City Council decision affecting the Housing Authority may also have a disqualifying effect on one 
or more of her interests. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
You are the City Attorney for the City of Tracy and the authorized represe,ntative of Tracy 
City Councilmember Rhodesia Ransom. In her private capacity, the Councilmember is the 
Executive Director of Sow A Seed Community Foundation (the "Foundation"), a local nonprofit 
organization that offers mentoring and seeks to improve the social, emotional, and behavioral health 
of youth in the County of San Joaquin. The Councilmember receives more than $500 in annual 
income for serving as the Executive Director of the Foundation. 
The Housing Authority of the County of San Joaquin ( the "Housing Authority") is a local 
government agency that provides housing to eligible County residents, including City of Tracy 
residents. The Housing Authority has proposed leasing office space within the City of Tracy to the 
Foundation. The proposed lease is for an undetermined number of years at a rate of $2,500 per 
month, and the Foundation's annual budget is $545,000. On June 14, 2019, you confirmed that the 
City of Tracy does not have authority or budgetary control over the Housing Authority. 
The Lease Agreement 
Section 87100 provides that "[n ]o public official at any level of state or local government 
shall make, participate in making or in any way use his official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest." An official's 
financial interests that may give rise to a disqualifying conflict of interest under the Act are 
identified in Section 87103. With respect to the Housing Authority's decisions relating to the lease 
agreement, the Councilmember has a source of income interest in the Foundation because she has 
aggregated more than $500 from it the past 12 months (see Section 87103(c)), and an interest in her 
personal finances and those of immediate family members (see Section 87103), based on the facts 
presented. 
· 
Regulation 18704 defines "making," "participating in," and "using official position to 
attempt to influence" a decision for purposes of the Act, and provides in pertinent part as follows: 


File No. A-19-102 
Page No. 3 
(a) Making a Decision. A public official makes a governmental decision if 
the official authorizes or directs any action, votes, appoints a person, obligates 
or commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any 
contractual agreement on behalf of his or her agency. 
(b) Participating in a Decision. A public official participates in a 
governmental decision if the official provides information, an opinion, or a 
recommendation for the purpose of affecting the decision without significant 
intervening substantive review. 
(c) Using Official Position to Attempt to Influence a Decision. A public 
official uses his or her official position to influence a governmental decision if 
he or she: 
(1) Contacts or appears before any official in his or her agency or in an 
agency subject to the authority or budgetary control of his or her agency for 
the purpose of affecting a decision; or 
(2) Contacts or appears before any official in any other government 
agency for the purpose of affecting a decision, and the public official acts or 
purports to act within his or her authority or on behalf of his or her agency in 
making the contact. 
With respect to Housing Authority decisions relating to the potential lease agreement 
between the Housing Aut~ority and the Foundation, the Councilmember would not be "making," 
"participating in" or "using (her) official position to influence" those decisions so long as she acts 
in her private capacity as the Executive Director of the Foundation and does not purport to act 
within her authority as a Tracy City CounciJmember or on behalf of the Tracy City Council. 
The Housing Authority 
As noted above, an official's interests that may give rise to a disqualifying conflict of 
interest under the Act are identified in Section 87103, and include, among others, an interest in a 
source of income or gifts, aggregating $500 or more in value in the 12 months prior to the decision 
at issue, and an interest in the official's personal finances and those of immediate family members. 
Based on the facts presented, the Councilmember would not have a financial interest in the 
Housing Authority under the Act's conflict of interest provisions as a result of the Foundation 
entering into the lease agreement with the Housing Authority. However, the Act's conflict of 
interest provisions apply on a case by case basis, and would prohibit the Councilmember from 
taking part in a governmental decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision would have a 
material financial effect on one or more of her financial interests distinguishable from the decision's 
effect on the public generally. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) The request for advice that was the 
basis for this letter does not specifically identify a City Council decision that may have a 
disqualifying effect on the Councilmember' s interests at issue. Therefore, the Councilmember 
should seek additional advice if there is any indication that a specifically identified City Council 
decision affecting the Housing Authority may also have a disqualifying effect on one or more of her 
interests, including her source of income interest in the Foundation or her interest in her personal 
finances. 


File No. A-19-102 
Page No. 4 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
MFC:sal 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
~-~ 
Mattbew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division

Authority?
sections.conclusion:
(1) No. The Act would not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the Housing 
Authority's governmental decisions relating to the lease agreement as the Executive Director of the 
Foundation because the Councilmember would not be "making," "participating in," or "using 
official position to attempt to influence" those decisions so long as she does not purport to act 
within her authority as a Tracy City Councilmember or on behalf of the Tracy City Council in

(2) The Act's conflict of interest provisions apply on a case by case basis, and would 
prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in a governmental decision if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision would have a material financial effect on one or more of her financial 
interests distinguishable from the decision's effect on the public generally. Accordingly, the 
Councilmember should seek additional advice if there is any indication that a specifically identified 
City Council decision affecting the Housing Authority may also have a disqualifying effect on one 
or more of her interests.

QUESTION: (1) Does the Act prohibit Councilmember Ransom, in her private capacity as Executive 
Director of the local nonprofit organization "Sow A Seed Community Foundation," from executing 
a lease agreement for office and programming space for the Foundation with the Housing Authority 
of the County of San Joaquin? 
(2) If the Foundation enters into the lease agreement with the Housing Authority, would the 
Councilmember be disqualified from future City Council decisions involving the Housing 
Authority?

CONCLUSION: (1) No. The Act would not prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in the Housing 
Authority's governmental decisions relating to the lease agreement as the Executive Director of the 
Foundation because the Councilmember would not be "making," "participating in," or "using 
official position to attempt to influence" those decisions so long as she does not purport to act 
within her authority as a Tracy City Councilmember or on behalf of the Tracy City Council in 
regard to those decisions. 
(2) The Act's conflict of interest provisions apply on a case by case basis, and would 
prohibit the Councilmember from taking part in a governmental decision if it is reasonably 
foreseeable that the decision would have a material financial effect on one or more of her financial 
interests distinguishable from the decision's effect on the public generally. Accordingly, the 
Councilmember should seek additional advice if there is any indication that a specifically identified 
City Council decision affecting the Housing Authority may also have a disqualifying effect on one 
or more of her interests.
